properties.name: Advice
properties.location: Shepparton, Seymour, Wodonga, Wangaratta, Echuca, Kerang and surrounds
properties.webBody:
The Bureau of Meteorology has issued a Severe Weather Warning for Shepparton, Seymour, Wodonga, Wangaratta, Echuca, Kerang and surrounds.
  • This Advice message is for heavy rainfall which may lead to flash flooding.
  • Six-hourly rainfall totals between 40 to 60 mm are likely.
  • Heavy rainfall is expected to ease by this evening.
Stay informed and be aware of potential hazards caused by the severe weather. Hazards include floodwater, debris, damaged buildings, trees down, and fallen power lines.
What you should do:
  • Park your car undercover and away from trees.
  • Stay inside if conditions outside appear dangerous.
  • Contact family members and neighbours to ensure they are aware of the situation, if safe to do so.
  • When preparing your property, ensure you take appropriate safety precautions and do so well before the severe weather arrives.
If you are driving:
  • If driving conditions are dangerous, safely pull over away from trees, drains, low lying areas and floodwater.
  • Be aware of road hazards such as mud, debris and damaged roads or bridges.
  • For road closure information, check the VicTraffic website, contact your local council or call VicRoads (13 11 70).
Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.

Call Triple Zero (000) in a life threatening emergency. For severe weather related emergency assistance from the SES, phone 132 500.
Impacts in your area:
  • 79 mm was recorded at Tutye in the 6 hours to 4:59 am. 
  • 48 mm was recorded at Kyabram in the 6 hours to 10:33am. 
  • 47.2 mm was recorded at Birchip in the 4 hours to 5:55 am. 
  • 51.6 mm was recorded at Mildura in the 4 hours and 30 minutes to 5:30 am. 
  • 59 mm was recorded at Walpeup in the 6 hours to 5:11 am.

The potential impacts of severe weather in your area include:
  • Flash flooding
  • Overflowing drains and gutters
  • Damage to vehicles
  • Flooding in properties and homes
  • Dangerous road conditions

This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 02/03/2026 05:30 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicstorms)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages, call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Community Information
properties.location: between the townships of  Irrewillipe, Kawarren, Lovat, Wimba, Charleys Creek, Carlisle River and covers the township of Gellibrand
properties.webBody:
This Community Information message is being issued for between the townships of Irrewillipe, Kawarren, Lovat, Wimba, Charleys Creek, Carlisle River and covers the township of Gellibrand. 
  • This Community Information replaces the message issued on 17 February 2026. 
  • There is a bushfire at Carlisle River that is under control.
  • Smoke may be visible from nearby communities and roads. 
  • Firefighters are currently patrolling this fire.  
  • Please observe all track closures in National Park and State Forest.    
There is currently no threat to communities however be aware of crews and heavy machinery working to rehabilitate affected tracks and stabilising areas of the fire ground.  
This message was issued by Forest Fire Management Victoria.

Use multiple sources to stay informed: 
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfires).
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.  
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact National Relay Service for assistance and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Watch & Act
properties.webBody:
This Flash Flood Watch and Act is being issued for Echuca, Tongala and Kyabram.

  • Heavy rainfall is causing widespread flash flooding.
  • A Severe Weather Warning for Heavy Rainfall is current for the area. Six-hourly rainfall totals between 40mm to 60mm are possible.
  • You can stay safe by avoiding flooded and low-lying areas. 
  • Floodwater can be very deep, rise quickly and move fast. 
  • Be aware that heavy rainfall may also increase the potential for landslides and debris across roads.
If you are located in the areas around Echuca, Tongala and Kyabram, you are in danger. You should immediately move indoors, away from floodwater. Do not enter floodwater.
What you should do: 
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never enter floodwater. 
If you are inside: 
  • Stay inside and be aware of rising floodwater. 
  • If floodwater comes inside, move to a higher point such as a kitchen bench or second story. 
  • Follow your emergency plan, if you have one. 
  • Contact family members and neighbours to ensure they are aware of the situation, if safe to do so. 
If you are outside: 
  • Stay away from trees, drains, low lying areas, creeks, canals, culverts and floodwater. 
  • Seek shelter indoors, away from floodwater. 
If you are driving: 
  • Do not drive through floodwater. 
  • Find alternative travel routes if roads or underpasses are flooded. 
  • Be aware of driving hazards, such as mud, debris, damaged roads and fallen trees. 
  • If driving conditions are dangerous, safely pull over away from trees, drains, low lying areas and floodwater. 
Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings. 

Call Triple Zero (000) in a life threatening emergency. For emergency assistance from the SES, call 132 500.
Impacts in your area: 
  • Many homes have been threatened by floodwater.
  • Heavy rainfall is expected to ease by this evening.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.   

The next update is expected by 02/03/2026 05:50 pm or as the situation changes.   

Use multiple sources to stay informed: 
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au 
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226 
  • VicEmergency app 
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods).
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages, call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.   
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for Avoca River to Charlton Town.

  • The Avoca River at Charlton Town is currently at 2.63m and steady, below the minor flood level.
  • Areas of widespread moderate to heavy rainfall with embedded thunderstorms are forecast to develop during Sunday, continuing into Monday.
  • This forecast rainfall is likely to cause river level rises across the Avoca River from Monday morning, with minor flooding (4.00m) possible at Charlton Town from Monday evening.
  • Higher levels are possible with forecast rainfall.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
  • Obey road closure signs. Plan ahead so you don't drive on flooded roads.
  • Don't drive, walk, swim or play in floodwater because it is dangerous.
  • Stay away from flooded drains, rivers, streams and waterways.
Impacts in your area: 
  • Areas around rivers and streams, including caravan parks and camping grounds, may become flooded if significant rainfall is received.
  • In the 48 hours to 9am Sunday, 1 March 28.6mm of rainfall was received at Yawong Weir. Since 9am this morning, 7.2mm has been recorded at Yawong Weir, and 9.4mm at Charlton.
  • Emergency services are closely monitoring the situation and will advise if any further action is required by the community.
  • This flood warning will be updated as required.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 02/03/2026 01:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.location: Central, North West and South West Victoria
properties.webBody:
A Final Flood Watch has been issued for Central, North West and South West Victoria.
  • Widespread riverine flooding is no longer expected with rain to slowly contract east across Victoria. 
  • Some moderate falls remain possible in the far northeast during Tuesday.
  • Rainfall totals are not expected to cause widespread riverine flooding, though localised flash flooding may still occur in areas of heavy rainfall.  
  • A Severe Weather Warning for heavy rainfall remains current for parts of Victoria. 
  • Flood warnings are current for the Avoca and Richardson rivers. 
The threat of flooding is reduced. You can now return to normal activity, but be aware of any hazards that may still exist in the warning area.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

This will be the last warning issued for this flood.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages, call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.location: Pemburthy, Woods Point, A1 Mine Settlement, Raspberry Point, View Point, Gaffneys Creek, Jericho, Matlock
properties.webBody:
This Advice message is being issued for the area surround Gaffney's Creek and Wood's Point.  There is a bushfire at Mansfield-Woods Point Rd, Gaffneys Creek that is now contained.

This Advice replaces the Advice message issued on Thursday the 26th February at 11.24am. This Advice replaces the [warning level] issued at [time].
  • There is currently no threat to communities, but you should continue to stay informed and monitor conditions. 
  • Monitor weather warnings, forecasts, and river levels through the VicEmergency website/app/hotline. 
What you should do: 
  • Monitor weather conditions and warnings. 
  • Review your fire survival plan. 
  • If you do not have a plan, decide what you will do if the situation changes.
Impacts in your area: 
The following roads are closed: 
  • Woods point Road is closed from Knockwood to Frenchmans Gap. 
  • German Spur Track
  • Gaffney's Creek Track
  • Frenchman's Gap Track.
  • Check the VicTraffic website (traffic.transport.vic.gov.au) or call 13 11 70 for road closures.
  • Firefighters, machinery and aircraft will continue to work on this fire. 
  • Emergency services will remain in the area. 
  • Be aware that in fire affected areas, rainfall runoff into waterways may contain debris such as ash, soil, trees and rocks.  Heavy rainfall may increase the potential for landslides and debris across roads. 

This message was issued by Forest Fire Management Victoria.

The next update is expected by 03/03/2026 10:30 am or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed: 
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#VicFires)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
  • Please click here to see the Newsletter
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact National Relay Service on 1800 555 677 and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
This is a Final Flash Flood Warning for Mildura and surrounds.

  • The immediate threat of flash flooding has passed. 
  • Dangerous hazards including floodwater, mud, debris, damaged roads and fallen trees may still be present.
  • This Advice message replaces the Watch and Act issued at 6.28am.
The threat of flooding is reduced. You can now return to normal activity, but be aware of any hazards that may still exist in the warning area.
What you should do: 
  • Floodwater is dangerous - do not enter floodwater. 
  • Find alternative travel routes if roads are flooded. 
  • Be aware of driving hazards, such as mud, debris, damaged roads and fallen trees. 
If your home or business has been flooded: 
  • Check for building damage before you enter. 
  • Have all electrical and gas equipment professionally tested before use. 
  • Clean and dry out the building as soon as possible. 
Call Triple Zero (000) in a life threatening emergency. For emergency assistance from the SES call 132 500.
Impacts in your area: 
  • Driving conditions may be dangerous due to flooded roads and debris on roads.
  • For road closure information or report road hazards, call VicRoads 13 11 70, check the VicTraffic website (www.traffic.transport.vic.gov.au), or contact your local council.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service. 

This will be the last warning issued for this flood. 

Flood information: 
  • Further flash flood information for some areas can be found in Local Flood Guides. 
  • For river heights check the Bureau of Meteorology website or phone 1300 659 217. 
Use multiple sources to stay informed: 
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au 
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226 
  • VicEmergency app 
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods) 
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV. 
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages, call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.   
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact National Relay Service on 1800 555 677 and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.location: Mildura, Swan Hill, Bendigo, Hopetoun and surrounds.
properties.webBody:
Severe Weather Warning is no longer occuring in parts of the Mallee, Wimmera, Northern Country, North Central and Central forecast districts.
  • This Advice message replaces the Watch and Act issued at 5:37 pm, Monday 2 March 2026.
  • A Severe Weather Warning for heavy rainfall which may lead to flash flooding, is current in Northern Country and parts of Mallee, North Central and North East Forecast Districts.
You can now return to normal activity as the threat is reduced. Be aware of any hazards that may still exist, such as floodwater, debris, damaged buildings, trees down, and fallen power lines.
What you should do: 
  • If your home or business has been flooded, check for building damage before you enter and have all electrical and gas equipment professionally tested before use.
  • Check your property for damage. 
  • Keep clear of damaged buildings, power lines and trees. 
If you are driving: 
  • Drive slowly and obey all road signs. Never drive through floodwater.
  • Be aware of road hazards including trees, floodwater and debris.
  • If driving conditions are dangerous, safely pull over away from trees, drains, low lying areas and floodwater.
Call Triple Zero (000) in a life threatening emergency. 

For severe weather related emergency assistance from the SES, phone 132 500.
Impacts in your area: 
The potential impacts of severe weather in your area include:
  • Flash flooding
  • Overflowing drains and gutters
  • Damage to vehicles
  • Flooding in properties and homes
  • Dangerous road conditions
This message was issued by State Emergency Service. 

This will be the final message issued for this incident. 

Use multiple sources to stay informed: 
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au 
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app 
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicstorms) 
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV. 
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.   
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for Avon-Richardson Rivers.

  • Moderate rainfall totals have been observed across the Avon and Richardson catchments over the weekend, with river level rises now occurring along parts of the Richardson River. Further rainfall is forecast for the remainder of Monday which may cause additional river level rises. With this forecast rainfall, minor flooding is possible at Donald Town from overnight Monday into Tuesday.
  • With forecast rainfall, the Richardson River at Donald Town may reach around the minor flood level (2.20m) from Monday afternoon. Higher levels are possible.
  • As a precautionary measure, a sandbag station is set up in Donald, at the Council Depot on Avon Place.
  • A Flood Watch is current for parts of Central, North West and South West Victoria, including the Wimmera and Avon-Richardson rivers. A Severe Weather Warning for heavy, locally intense rainfall, is current for parts of Victoria.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
  • Obey road closure signs. Plan ahead so you don't drive on flooded roads.
  • Don't drive, walk, swim or play in floodwater because it is dangerous.
  • Stay away from flooded drains, rivers, streams and waterways.
Impacts in your area: 
  • Areas around rivers and streams, including caravan parks and camping grounds, may become flooded if significant rainfall is received.
  • Minor flooding is possible along the Richardson River.The Richardson River at Donald Town is currently at 0.43 m and rising, below the minor flood level. With forecast rainfall, the Richardson River at Donald Town may reach around the minor flood level (2.20 m) from overnight Monday into Tuesday.
  • Victoria State Emergency Service and Buloke Shire Council are closely monitoring the situation and will advise if any further action is required by the community.
  • Residents who are concerned about potential flooding can collect sandbags from today from the Council Depot on Avon Place. Please bring your own shovel and trailer or ute.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 02/03/2026 03:50 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.
